summary: Matched `fn_800DC070` in `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Attack100.c`, replacing an unmatched stub comment with the CaptureJump enter function. The PR was closed without merge and had no visible review discussion in the provided slice, so the implementation details are evidence-grounded from the PR body and diff rather than reviewer confirmation.

key_files.role: Implemented previously stubbed `fn_800DC070` as the CaptureJump enter routine.

what_changed: Replaced `/// #fn_800DC070` with a C implementation of `void fn_800DC070(Fighter_GObj* gobj)`.
Fetched the fighter with `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` and cached `fp->victim_gobj` as `victim` before resetting common state.
Called `ftCommon_8007D5D4(fp)` to reset common fighter state.
Set launch velocity for the attacker: `self_vel.x = -facing_dir * p_ftCommonData->x374` and `self_vel.y = p_ftCommonData->x378`.
Called `ftCo_800DC920(victim, gobj)` before transitioning motion state.
Transitioned the fighter into `ftCo_MS_CaptureJump` using `Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, ftCo_MS_CaptureJump, 0, 0.0F, 1.0F, 0.0F, NULL)`.
smart_moves: Cached `victim_gobj` before calling `ftCommon_8007D5D4(fp)`, which is important because common-state reset helpers may mutate fighter fields; the diff preserves access to the victim object needed later by `ftCo_800DC920`.
Used existing project idioms and symbols: `GET_FIGHTER`, `Fighter_GObj`, `p_ftCommonData`, `ftCo_MS_CaptureJump`, and `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`.
Kept the implementation small and direct, matching the behavior described by the PR body rather than adding speculative abstractions.
Used named motion-state enum `ftCo_MS_CaptureJump` instead of the raw state value `0xE6` mentioned in the PR summary.
decomp_lessons: For enter-state functions, the operation order can matter for matching and correctness: read dependent object pointers first, perform reset/setup helpers, update movement/state variables, call release/react helpers, then change motion state.
When a common reset helper is involved, preserve any needed pre-reset fighter fields in locals if they are consumed later.
Motion-state transitions in ftCommon should prefer existing enum names and canonical `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` argument patterns when available.
Global common-data fields such as `p_ftCommonData->x374` and `x378` may encode state-specific velocity constants even when field names are still anonymous.

The PR reports `fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0`, but because the PR was closed unmerged and no review comments are present, future work should verify the function against current assembly before reusing it wholesale.
naming_conventions: `fn_800DC070` remained address-based, indicating no stable semantic name was introduced in this slice.
The local variable for `fp->victim_gobj` was named `victim`, a concise semantic name matching the PR body's description of the escaping/buried fighter.
Motion state used the existing enum-style name `ftCo_MS_CaptureJump`.
The move-state field remained anonymous/partially named as `fp->mv.co.buryjump.x0`, showing that struct-field naming was not resolved by this PR.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Implemented the exact control-flow sequence for a state enter function: load fighter, save victim, reset common state, assign velocities, zero move-state counter, call release helper, then change motion state.
Used float literals matching common decomp style: `0.0F` and `1.0F`.
Passed `NULL` as the final `Fighter_ChangeMotionState` argument and used zeroed start/blend values consistent with many matched state-transition calls.
Computed backward horizontal velocity with unary negation of `facing_dir` multiplied by a common-data constant, matching the described launch-back behavior.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was closed with `merged_at: null`; the slice does not explain why it was closed.
follow_up_queries: Check whether `fn_800DC070` was later merged or reimplemented in another PR.
Search for later naming of `fn_800DC070` or `mv.co.buryjump.x0` to replace address-based and anonymous field names.
Verify current assembly for `fn_800DC070` because this PR was closed unmerged despite reporting a 100.0 fuzzy match.
Compare nearby CaptureJump functions such as `ftCo_CaptureJump_Anim` and helper `ftCo_800DC920` for consistent ordering and naming.
